name: Zucchini Stuffed with Tomato
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon Dried dill weed (weed, not seed)
1/4 cup Freshly-grated Parmesan or Romano cheese
Salt and freshly-ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut both ends from the zucchini, then cut them in half crosswise through the middle.
2. Scoop out most of the pulp from one end, reserving the pulp for a later soup. Leave a round tube of squash sealed at the other end.
3. Set aside.
4. Heat a frying pan and add the olive oil. Add the garlic and onion; sauté until the onions are clear.
5. Add the tomatoes, red wine, dill weed, sugar, salt, and black pepper. Cook for about 5 minutes or until the tomatoes are limp but not mushy.
6. Stand the squash baskets on one end in a baking dish large enough to hold them. A glass pie plate works well.
7. Fill each basket with the tomato mixture and top with cheese.
8. Pour white wine around the bottoms of the zucchini.
9. Bake, uncovered, at 400°F until tender but firm, about 35 minutes.
